rationale.summary: Vote: NEEDS_MORE_INFO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (active), critical information needed for a responsible directional vote is still missing. Decisive reason: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "Mithril uses stake-based threshold multi-signatures to certify Cardano state in a trustless way so nodes and applications can verify state rapidly without relying on the entire chain." and clears the evidence gates. What would enable a directional vote: Provide: cost comparables, budget proportionality, or sustainability evidence; Provide independent evidence for: Full node synchronisation is slow today and light clients rely on centralized trust. Residual uncertainty: Treasury flow signal is in stressed regime (total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals). Confidence context: score 0.21; confidence 77.2%.
rationale.summary_raw: DRep ratification support is material but below threshold; treated as a modest context signal.
top_fixes: Provide: cost comparables, budget proportionality, or sustainability evidence
Provide independent evidence for: Full node synchronisation is slow today and light clients rely on centralized trust.
missing_evidence: cost comparables, budget proportionality, or sustainability evidence

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund Mithril Protocol continued development through the Intersect budget process.

Recipient: 2026 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract stake address stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v, submitted by Intersect on behalf of the vendor; vendor identified as Teragone in the funding disclosure.
Deliverables: Mithril Protocol Continuous Development, Intersect budget administration and oversight, Milestone-based disbursement controls through TRSC/PSSC smart contracts

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Mithril uses stake-based threshold multi-signatures to certify Cardano state in a trustless way so nodes and applications can verify state rapidly without relying on the entire chain.
Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Full node synchronisation is slow today and light clients rely on centralized trust.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Funding Mithril continued development through Intersect MBO is presented as preserving Cardano decentralization while supporting scaling.
Claim (governance, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposal achieved the required 67% support threshold during the 2026 Intersect Budget Process Hydra Voting phase.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The requested 3,810,423 ada consists of 3,699,440 ada for Mithril Protocol Continuous Development and 110,983 ada for the Intersect Budget Administration fee.

Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposer states the withdrawal does not breach the applicable 350M Net Change Limit at the time of submission, either on its own or in aggregate.
missing: Independent evidence for: Full node synchronisation is slow today and light clients rely on centralized trust.
Independent evidence for: Funding Mithril continued development through Intersect MBO is presented as preserving Cardano decentralization while supporting scaling.
Independent evidence for: Teragone has not previously received direct funding from Cardano treasury or community governance programs, while prior Mithril work was funded by IOG.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Mithril uses stake-based threshold multi-signatures to certify Cardano state in a trustless way so nodes and applications can verify state rapidly without relying on the entire chain." and clears the evidence gates.
Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Full node synchronisation is slow today and light clients rely on centralized trust." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.

actionable_asks: Publish a line-item budget: what is being bought, at what unit cost.
State the cost against the benefit: what does this buy, per ada spent.
Describe the funding path beyond this grant, or say plainly that there isn't one.
Tie payment to defined deliverables, not to elapsed time or headcount.
State your AI-cost assumption: this work is cheaper to produce than it was, and the price should show it.
missing: cost comparables, budget proportionality, or sustainability evidence

decision_contract.composite_formula: 40% ecosystem benefit + 25% delivery confidence + 20% cost efficiency + 15% downside protection - 0.50
decision_contract.benefit_formula: 18% critical infrastructure + 8% open source + 15% adoption + 12% ecosystem leverage + 10% strategic necessity + 10% prior delivery + 10% established service + 8% builder dependency + 5% low substitutability + 4% non-funding disruption risk
decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.

flags.detail: Treasury withdrawal to a single address

treasury_capacity.caveat: Set by an Info governance action. Info actions change no ledger state and can NEVER be enacted -- they always expire -- so no formally ratified NCL exists or can exist. This is the strongest available public chain evidence: an anchor document byte-verified against its on-chain blake2b-256 hash, covering the current epoch, which won a majority of participating DRep stake. It is NOT enacted ledger state, and the majority may fall short of the supermajority other action types require. Published with every treasury rationale that relies on it.
